description: A controlled action commits successfully while emitting no audit record, leaving the change invisible to any review.
body: Failure mode: a path that modifies a regulated resource writes the business change but skips the audit record (logging is best-effort, outside the transaction, or omitted), so the action leaves no attributable trace. Detection signals: performing the action produces a data change with no matching audit row; audit writes are fire-and-forget and can fail silently; the audit sink lacks records for known actions. Do not treat application logs or async best-effort logging as the audit trail. Safer fallback: write the audit record in the same transaction as the change with actor, time, action, and before/after state, and add a test asserting exactly one immutable record per controlled action.
